text: How is it that ye sought me — wist ye not that I must be about my Father's business?
It is written — man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God
Get thee behind me, Satan — thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve
It is said — thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God
The Spirit of the Lord is upon me — anointed to preach the gospel to the poor, heal the brokenhearted, and proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord
This day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears
No prophet is accepted in his own country — Elijah was sent to a widow of Sidon, and only Naaman the Syrian was cleansed
Hold thy peace, and come out of him — Jesus rebukes the unclean spirit
I must preach the kingdom of God to other cities also — for therefore am I sent
Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught
Fear not — from henceforth thou shalt catch men
I will — be thou clean; go shew thyself to the priest and offer for thy cleansing as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them
Follow me — Jesus calls Levi the publican from the receipt of custom
Have ye not read what David did when hungered — he ate the shewbread which was lawful only for the priests
Rise up and stand forth — is it lawful on the sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or destroy it? Stretch forth thy hand
Why call ye me, Lord, Lord — and do not the things which I say?
I have not found so great faith — no, not in Israel — Jesus marvels at the centurion
Weep not — young man, I say unto thee, arise — Jesus raises the widow's son at Nain

This generation is like children in the marketplace — John came fasting and they said he had a devil; the Son of man came eating and they called him a glutton; but wisdom is justified of all her children
Two debtors forgiven by a creditor — the one forgiven most loves most; this woman has loved much, while Simon offered no water, kiss, or oil
My mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of God and do it — let us go over unto the other side of the lake
What is thy name? — Jesus questions the demoniac at Gadara
Return to thine own house, and shew how great things God hath done unto thee
Who touched me? — somebody hath touched me, for I perceive that virtue is gone out of me
Daughter, be of good comfort — thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace
Weep not — she is not dead, but sleepeth
Maid, arise — Jesus raises Jairus' daughter
Give ye them to eat — make them sit down by fifties in a company
Whom say the people that I am? — Jesus questions his disciples while praying alone
O faithless and perverse generation — how long shall I be with you and suffer you? Bring thy son hither
Forbid him not — he that is not against us is for us
Ye know not what manner of spirit ye are of — the Son of man is not come to destroy men's lives, but to save them

I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven — rejoice not that spirits are subject to you, but that your names are written in heaven; the Father has hid these things from the wise and revealed them to babes
What is written in the law? — how readest thou? — Jesus questions the lawyer
Woe unto you, Pharisees — for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues and greetings in the markets
Woe unto you also, ye lawyers — for ye lade men with grievous burdens and touch them not with one of your fingers
The wisdom of God sends prophets and apostles whom this generation shall slay — woe to lawyers who took away the key of knowledge and hindered those entering
Man, who made me a judge or a divider over you?
Let your loins be girded and your lights burning — blessed are servants whom the lord finds watching, for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not
But I have a baptism to be baptized with — and how am I straitened till it be accomplished
When ye see a cloud rise out of the west ye say a shower comes; when the south wind blows ye say there will be heat — yet ye discern not this time
Suppose ye that these Galileans were sinners above all the Galileans because they suffered such things?
Or those eighteen on whom the tower in Siloam fell — think ye that they were sinners above all in Jerusalem?
Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity — Jesus heals on the sabbath
Thou hypocrite — doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or ass from the stall and lead him to watering?
When the master shuts the door ye will say we ate and drank in thy presence — but he shall say, I know you not whence ye are; depart from me, all workers of iniquity
Tell that fox I cast out devils today and tomorrow — O Jerusalem, how often would I have gathered thy children as a hen gathers her brood, and ye would not
Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath day?
Ye justify yourselves before men, but God knoweth your hearts — that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God
It is easier for heaven and earth to pass than one tittle of the law to fail
When a servant comes from the field the master does not thank him — so likewise, when ye have done all things commanded, say, We are unprofitable servants who have done our duty
Go shew yourselves unto the priests — and as the ten lepers went, they were cleansed
Were there not ten cleansed — but where are the nine? None returned to give glory to God save this stranger
The days will come when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man and shall not — go not after them when they say, See here or see there
The Son of man shall be delivered to the Gentiles, mocked, scourged, and put to death — and the third day he shall rise again
What wilt thou that I shall do unto thee? — receive thy sight; thy faith hath saved thee
Zacchaeus, make haste and come down — for to day I must abide at thy house
This day is salvation come to this house — forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham
Go into the village and find a colt tied whereon never man sat — loose him and bring him; if any ask, say the Lord hath need of him
I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out
If thou hadst known the things that belong unto thy peace — but now they are hid from thine eyes; thine enemies shall lay thee even with the ground because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation
It is written, my house is the house of prayer — but ye have made it a den of thieves
I will also ask you one thing — the baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?
Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things
How say they that Christ is David's son — when David himself in the Psalms calls him Lord, saying, Sit thou on my right hand till I make thine enemies thy footstool?
As for these things which ye behold, the days will come — there shall not be left one stone upon another that shall not be thrown down
Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat
When ye are entered the city, a man bearing a pitcher of water shall meet you — follow him, and the goodman of the house will show you a large upper room furnished; there make ready
Behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table — the Son of man goeth as determined, but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed
Peter, the cock shall not crow this day before thou shalt thrice deny me — now he that hath a purse let him take it, for the scripture concerning me must be accomplished; it is enough
Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
Suffer ye thus far — be ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves? This is your hour, and the power of darkness
Thou sayest it — Jesus answers Pilate concerning being King of the Jews
Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves and your children — if they do these things in a green tree, what shall be done in the dry?
What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?
What things? — Jesus draws out the Emmaus disciples' account of Jesus of Nazareth
O fools, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken — ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?
Peace be unto you — Jesus stands among the disciples after the resurrection
Have ye here any meat? — the risen Jesus asks for food to confirm his bodily presence
